question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single GET request to '/metrics' and receive the complete Prometheus text format output including all histogram bucket boundaries defined in sizeBuckets and requestDurationHistogram, revealing internal performance thresholds and SLA targets configured by operators?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a maximum label value length check on the 'route' label in requestBytesHistogram to send requests with kilobyte-length URL paths, causing each Prometheus label set to consume disproportionate memory per observation?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that are never completed (connection hang), keeping requestInflightGauge permanently incremented for a given route/method pair, causing the gauge to diverge from reality and misleading autoscaling or alerting systems into incorrect responses?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the '/metrics' endpoint to determine the exact histogram bucket counts for 'hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_bytes' with sizeBuckets [512, 1024, 10240, 25600, 51200], inferring the distribution of request body sizes and identifying which endpoints accept large payloads for targeted abuse?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a crafted URL that matches no registered route, causing the RouteMatcher to return an empty or default route string that gets recorded as a label value in all four histograms/gauges, polluting metric data with noise that degrades observability?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a POST request to '/metrics' (which only registers GET via Routes()) and observe whether the server returns a 405 Method Not Allowed or silently ignores it, leaking information about the routing framework's behavior and potentially bypassing method restrictions in misconfigured deployments?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of requests per second to increment all four metric vectors simultaneously (requestBytesHistogram, requestDurationHistogram, requestInflightGauge, responseBytesHistogram), causing lock contention in the Prometheus client's internal mutex-protected label maps and degrading request throughput for all users?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the unauthenticated '/metrics' endpoint to monitor 'hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight' in real time and detect when the server is processing sensitive operations (e.g., large transaction submissions), timing follow-up requests to collide with in-progress state mutations?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that MetricsMiddleware() wraps 'next' with middleware.Instrument without validating that 'next' is non-nil, causing a nil pointer dereference in the type assertion 'next.(middleware.RouteMatcher)' if a nil handler is passed, crashing the server?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the '/metrics' endpoint to extract the 'hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_duration' histogram with buckets [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5] seconds per route, identifying which routes have p99 latencies above 5 seconds and are therefore most vulnerable to slowloris or resource exhaustion attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with HTTP methods that are not in the standard set (e.g., arbitrarily long custom method strings) to create new label combinations in requestDurationHistogram's 'method' dimension, causing the histogram's internal time-series count to grow without bound?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial of service at process startup by exploiting a race condition in the init() function's MustRegister calls on prometheus.DefaultRegisterer if the package is loaded concurrently in a test or plugin environment, causing a panic from duplicate registration?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the '/metrics' endpoint to confirm the exact value of the 'application' constant label ('rosetta') attached to all metrics via prometheus.WrapRegistererWith, leaking the application identity and enabling targeted fingerprinting of the deployment?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with response bodies larger than the largest sizeBucket (50*1024 bytes) to cause all responseBytesHistogram observations to fall in the '+Inf' bucket, making the histogram useless for capacity planning and masking abnormally large response sizes from operators?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET request to '/metrics' with a very large Accept header or query string to probe whether promhttp.Handler() performs any input validation, potentially triggering a buffer overflow or excessive memory allocation in the HTTP header parsing layer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum cardinality limit on the 'route' label in requestInflightGauge to create thousands of unique gauge time-series by sending requests to thousands of distinct URL paths, exhausting Prometheus client memory and causing OOM kills?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the '/metrics' endpoint to observe changes in 'hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_inflight' gauge values over time to infer the concurrency level of other users' requests, enabling side-channel inference of system load and user activity patterns?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the middleware.Instrument.Wrap() call to record observations on all four metric vectors for every single request, and by sending millions of requests, cause the Prometheus client's internal histogram bucket arrays to consume gigabytes of memory?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the '/metrics' endpoint to extract the total count and sum from 'hiero_mirror_rosetta_request_bytes' histograms, computing the average request body size per route and identifying endpoints that process financial transaction data based on characteristic payload sizes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a 'ws' label value of 'true' vs 'false' in requestDurationHistogram by manipulating WebSocket upgrade headers, causing the histogram to split time-series by WebSocket status and potentially leaking whether WebSocket connections are supported on specific routes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the '/metrics' endpoint to determine the exact number of HTTP requests processed per route (from histogram _count fields), enabling precise enumeration of which API endpoints are actively used and which are dormant, aiding targeted attack surface mapping?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with URL paths containing null bytes or other control characters that get passed as 'route' label values to requestBytesHistogram, causing the Prometheus text exposition format to produce malformed output that breaks downstream metric parsers or monitoring systems?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent GET requests to '/metrics' at a rate exceeding the Prometheus handler's ability to serialize the registry, causing promhttp.Handler() to block on internal locks and starve other goroutines waiting to record metric observations, degrading overall application performance?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: MetricsMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that requestDurationHistogram uses fixed buckets [0.1, 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2.5, 5] seconds to infer that requests taking longer than 5 seconds are not distinguished, and deliberately craft slow requests that all fall in '+Inf', masking the true latency distribution from operators?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/metrics.go -> Scope: '] [Function: Routes()] Can an unprivileged external user use the '/metrics' endpoint to observe the 'hiero_mirror_rosetta_response_bytes' histogram and infer the size of error responses vs. success responses per route, leaking information about internal error handling behavior and response structure?
